title: Deinandra halliana
taxonomy: Deinandra halliana is a plant species classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the tribe Madinae and the genus Deinandra, which is a group of flowering plants native to California and Baja California. The species is further classified as Deinandra halliana (Greene) B.G. Bendicz.
uses: Deinandra halliana, also known as Hall's tarweed, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Research suggests that the plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may be beneficial in treating various health issues. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
